name: Back — Flexion
f1: Rectus Abdominis, Internal Oblique, External Oblique

name: Back — Extension
f1: Erector Spinae, Quadratus Lumborum
f2: Rectus Abdominis, Internal Oblique, External Oblique

name: Back — Right Lateral Flexion
f1: (R) Erector Spinae, (R) Quadratus Lumborum, (R) Internal Oblique, (R) External Oblique
f2: (L) Erector Spinae, (L) Quadratus Lumborum, (L) Internal Oblique, (L) External Oblique

name: Back — Left Lateral Flexion
f1: (L) Erector Spinae, (L) Quadratus Lumborum, (L) Internal Oblique, (L) External Oblique
f2: (R) Erector Spinae, (R) Quadratus Lumborum, (R) Internal Oblique, (R) External Oblique

name: Back — Right Rotation
f1: (R) Internal Oblique + (L) External Oblique

name: Back — Left Rotation
f1: (L) Internal Oblique + (R) External Oblique

name: Hip — Flexion
f1: Iliopsoas, Tensor Fasciae Latae, Gluteus Medius, Adductors

name: Hip — Extension
f1: Gluteus Maximus, Gluteus Medius
f2: Iliopsoas, Tensor Fasciae Latae, Gluteus Medius, Adductors

name: Hip — Abduction
f1: Gluteus Medius, Tensor Fasciae Latae, Gluteus Maximus, Piriformis

name: Hip — Adduction
f1: Gluteus Maximus (lower fibers), Adductors
f2: Gluteus Medius, Tensor Fasciae Latae, Gluteus Maximus, Piriformis

name: Hip — Lateral Rotation
f1: Piriformis, Gluteus Maximus, Iliopsoas (assists), Gluteus Medius

name: Hip — Medial Rotation
f1: Tensor Fasciae Latae, Gluteus Medius
f2: Piriformis, Gluteus Maximus, Iliopsoas, Gluteus Medius

name: Wrist — Flexion
f1: Flexor Carpi Radialis

name: Wrist — Extension
f1: Extensor Digitorum

name: Wrist — Abduction
f1: Flexor Carpi Radialis

name: Ankle — Dorsiflexion
f1: Extensor Digitorum Longus

name: Ankle — Plantarflexion
f1: Tibialis Posterior, Fibularis/Peroneus Longus

name: Foot — Inversion
f1: Tibialis Posterior

name: Foot — Eversion
f1: Fibularis/Peroneus Longus, Extensor Digitorum Longus

name: Process
f1: A broad designation for any bump or projection on a bone.
f2: mastoid process, xiphoid process, coracoid process

name: Spine
f1: An abrupt or pointed bony projection.
f2: spine of the scapula, anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS)

name: Trochanter
f1: A large, blunt bony process.
f2: greater and lesser trochanter of the femur

name: Tubercle
f1: A small rounded bump on a bone.
f2: greater and lesser tubercles of the humerus

name: Tuberosity
f1: A large, often rough and irregular bump on a bone.

name: Line
f1: A low ridge on a bone that doesn't stick up very far.

name: Crest
f1: A prominent, sharp ridge on a bone.

name: Head
f1: A large rounded end of a long bone, usually set off from the shaft by a narrower 'neck.'
f2: head of the humerus, head of the femur

name: Condyle
f1: A rounded end of a long bone, usually NOT set off by a neck, and typically smaller than a head.
f2: medial and lateral condyles of the femur

name: Epicondyle
f1: A bump located directly above (epi-) a condyle.
f2: medial and lateral epicondyles of the humerus

name: Facet
f1: A smooth, mostly flat articular surface on a bone.

name: Fossa
f1: A basin-like depression or hollow in a bone.

name: Foramen
f1: A hole or opening through a bone (Latin for 'opening').
f2: foramen magnum of the skull, foramina of the sacrum

name: Notch
f1: A deep indentation or cut-out in the edge of a bone.

name: Angle
f1: A point where two bony edges diverge from a common point.
f2: superior and inferior angles of the scapula
